Case_Title: Home layout looks correct on phone tablet and desktop
Steps_Summary: Resize the browser from phone width to tablet to desktop, including French and English if applicable
Expected_Result: Nothing overlaps in a confusing way; header, footer, and banners stay readable and match the design that was signed off

Case_Title: Keyboard users can move through header search and cart
Steps_Summary: Use only the Tab key to move through the header, search, and cart areas
Expected_Result: Each focused control shows a visible focus ring or outline, and focus never gets stuck where you cannot leave

Case_Title: Home page shows locale in the URL and main content
Steps_Summary: Open the home page
Expected_Result: The page loads, the main content region is visible, and the address bar shows the correct language segment (for example /fr or /en)
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-home.spec.ts

Case_Title: Header navigation links work on desktop
Steps_Summary: Open the home page on a desktop-sized window, then look at the top navigation
Expected_Result: Links such as Home, Products, and Cart appear and match what the shop should show
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-navigation.spec.ts

Case_Title: Editorial "Coup de cœur" page opens correctly
Steps_Summary: Open the Coup de cœur page from the menu or direct link
Expected_Result: The top banner or title area is visible so visitors know they are on the right page
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-static-pages.spec.ts -g "Coup de c"

Case_Title: FAQ page shows title and main answers area
Steps_Summary: Open the FAQ page
Expected_Result: Both the page title or hero and the main FAQ content block are visible
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-static-pages.spec.ts -g FAQ

Case_Title: Privacy policy page shows a clear title
Steps_Summary: Open the privacy policy page
Expected_Result: The title or hero at the top of the page is visible
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-static-pages.spec.ts -g Privacy

Case_Title: API returns the category tree for the shop
Steps_Summary: Call GET /api/categories as described in the automated test
Expected_Result: The server answers with HTTP 200 and a JSON structure that represents categories in a tree

Case_Title: API returns products belonging to a category
Steps_Summary: Call the category products endpoint as in the automated test
Expected_Result: The server answers with HTTP 200 and a list of products (the list may be empty)

Case_Title: Large category with many pages and filters
Steps_Summary: Move through several pages, change sort order, and try filters
Expected_Result: Pages load in a reasonable time, the browser console stays clean of serious errors, and the product order matches what you selected

Case_Title: Shop page lists products without errors
Steps_Summary: Open the shop or catalog page
Expected_Result: A product grid or list appears and the page does not show a broken or blank state
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-shop.spec.ts

Case_Title: Shop filter by Ambiance then add product to cart
Steps_Summary: Open the shop, open filters, pick the Ambiance filter, open a matching product, add it to the cart
Expected_Result: The cart shows that product and reflects the Ambiance choice where the UI is meant to show it
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/e2e/shop-filter-to-cart.spec.ts

Case_Title: API returns product details for a valid slug and 404 for a bad one
Steps_Summary: Call the product-by-slug endpoint once with a real slug and once with a slug that does not exist
Expected_Result: A real slug returns HTTP 200 with product data; a fake slug returns HTTP 404

Case_Title: Related products block on the product page
Steps_Summary: Scroll to the related or recommended products section
Expected_Result: You see up to four sensible related cards, and the current product is not offered as a link to itself

Case_Title: Product page shows an enabled Add to cart button
Steps_Summary: Open the product detail page for the test product configured in the environment
Expected_Result: The main Add to cart button is on screen, not hidden, and can be clicked
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-pdp.spec.ts

Case_Title: Shop sorting beyond what automated smoke covers
Steps_Summary: Try Featured order, price low to high, and price high to low
Expected_Result: The order of product cards on the page matches the sort option you chose

Case_Title: Search panel opens from the header on desktop
Steps_Summary: Click the search icon or control in the header
Expected_Result: A search sheet or panel opens and you can see a text field to type in
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-search.spec.ts

Case_Title: Search finds a product and it lands in the cart
Steps_Summary: Type a search query, open the first result, then add that product to the cart
Expected_Result: The mini cart or cart drawer shows the product you picked from search
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/e2e/search-and-shop-to-cart.spec.ts

Case_Title: Cart contents survive a full browser refresh
Steps_Summary: Put items in the cart, refresh the whole page with F5 or reload, then open the cart again
Expected_Result: The same products and quantities appear as before the refresh

Case_Title: Add to cart opens the drawer with a checkout path
Steps_Summary: On a product page click Add to cart
Expected_Result: The cart drawer opens and you see a clear way to continue toward checkout

Case_Title: Empty cart message when nothing was added
Steps_Summary: With an empty cart, open the cart from the header
Expected_Result: You see an empty-cart message or illustration, not leftover lines from an old session

Case_Title: Change quantity in cart and remove a line completely
Steps_Summary: Add a product, open the cart drawer, use plus and minus, then remove the line
Expected_Result: Quantities update correctly; after removal the cart shows the empty state if nothing remains
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/e2e/cart-qty-and-remove.spec.ts

Case_Title: API returns a shipping quote or a clear validation response
Steps_Summary: Send a POST for a shipping quote with valid or empty cart data as the test describes
Expected_Result: You get HTTP 200 with a quote when the input is valid, or HTTP 422 when the API is designed to reject the request

Case_Title: Shipping totals match each carrier choice
Steps_Summary: Build a cart that uses weight-based rules, then select each shipping carrier in turn
Expected_Result: The money amounts shown match the business rules for that carrier

Case_Title: Checkout with an empty cart shows a helpful state
Steps_Summary: Go to the checkout URL while the cart has no items
Expected_Result: The page explains that the cart is empty and offers a way back to shopping

Case_Title: Free-shipping promo around the minimum order amount
Steps_Summary: Add and remove lines so the cart total crosses the free-shipping threshold
Expected_Result: The free-shipping benefit turns on only when the cart is over the minimum, and turns off when it drops below

Case_Title: Promo codes at checkout valid invalid and edge cases
Steps_Summary: At checkout, enter working codes, wrong codes, and codes that should fail
Expected_Result: Discounts and error messages follow the business rules you were given

Case_Title: Cash-on-delivery instructions on the thank-you page
Steps_Summary: Read the body text of the thank-you page after placing a COD order
Expected_Result: The wording about paying on delivery matches what legal or operations approved

Case_Title: Guest pays cash on delivery from home to thank-you page
Preconditions: Front+API up; PLAYWRIGHT_ORIGIN; product slug in stock
Steps_Summary: From the home page, add a product, go through checkout as a guest, enter address, choose cash on delivery, and submit
Expected_Result: You land on a thank-you page whose web address and visible title match the active language
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/e2e/order-cod-free-shipping.spec.ts

Case_Title: Payment gateway callback URL responds as specified
Steps_Summary: Call the CMI callback route exactly as the automated test describes
Expected_Result: Each scenario returns the HTTP status code the test expects

Case_Title: Card payment in the CMI test environment in a real browser
Steps_Summary: Complete a full card payment flow in the sandbox, including return to the shop
Expected_Result: You see a clear paid or failed outcome, and sensitive values never appear in the browser address bar

Case_Title: API confirms an order when the reference is valid
Steps_Summary: Call GET order confirmation with a good reference and with a bad one as in the test
Expected_Result: Valid reference returns 200 with order data; invalid reference returns 404 according to the rules

Case_Title: Order reference matches between page and email
Steps_Summary: Place a COD order, then compare the thank-you page and the confirmation email
Expected_Result: The same order reference appears in both places with no mismatch

Case_Title: Marketing wording review in French and English
Steps_Summary: Walk through key texts on home and product pages with the product owner
Expected_Result: No blocking typos; tone feels acceptable for both locales

Case_Title: SEO canonical and alternate language tags on product pages
Preconditions: GSC or live FR EN PDP
Steps_Summary: View the HTML head of the same product in French and in English
Expected_Result: Canonical and hreflang tags point to the right URLs for each language version

Case_Title: Switching currency updates header and prices
Steps_Summary: Change currency from the header, then look at the header and at a product card
Expected_Result: The header shows the new currency code and at least one product price is formatted for that currency
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/e2e/currency-switch.spec.ts

Case_Title: Switching language between French and English
Steps_Summary: From the home page, open the language menu and pick the other language
Expected_Result: The URL prefix and navigation labels match the language you chose
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/e2e/locale-switch.spec.ts

Case_Title: API returns public brand information
Steps_Summary: Call GET /api/brand
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 with JSON that includes brand fields such as logo and colors

Case_Title: Brand logo and colors on real phones and tablets
Steps_Summary: Open home and a product page on iPhone Safari and Android Chrome
Expected_Result: Logo is sharp, colors match the brand guide, and the page does not flash the wrong theme

Case_Title: API returns a paginated list of products
Steps_Summary: Call GET /api/products with a small page size as in the test
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 with a products array and pagination metadata in the JSON

Case_Title: API rejects invalid checkout data with a clear error
Steps_Summary: POST checkout with a body that breaks validation rules
Expected_Result: HTTP 422 and a structured list of validation errors, not a generic server crash

Case_Title: Extra odd checkout requests beyond the automated suite
Preconditions: Laravel running optional Burp or Postman collection
Steps_Summary: Send malformed or edge-case bodies with Postman or similar
Expected_Result: The server never returns HTTP 500; answers stay consistent with validation or 422

Case_Title: API accepts or rejects contact form submissions correctly
Steps_Summary: POST the contact endpoint with valid data and again with invalid data
Expected_Result: Valid submission returns 201 when expected; invalid data returns 422 with clear rules

Case_Title: Contact flood or rate limit if the product enables it
Steps_Summary: Send many contact submissions in a short time
Expected_Result: The site slows you down, shows captcha, or blocks abuse according to the documented rules

Case_Title: Contact page shows the form on screen
Steps_Summary: Navigate to the contact page
Expected_Result: The contact form fields and submit control are visible
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/smoke/storefront-contact.spec.ts

Case_Title: Contact form can be submitted successfully
Steps_Summary: Open contact, fill required fields with valid values, submit
Expected_Result: A success message or status appears with wording that matches the intended copy
Run_Command: cd QA/playwright && npx playwright test tests/e2e/contact-form-submit.spec.ts

Case_Title: API logs an admin user in or refuses bad credentials
Steps_Summary: POST admin login with correct and incorrect passwords as in the test
Expected_Result: Good password returns a token or session payload; bad password returns 401

Case_Title: Admin web app login session and logout
Preconditions: Admin Next URL and test users
Steps_Summary: Sign in to the admin app, open one screen, then sign out completely
Expected_Result: After logout, protected admin pages or APIs respond as unauthenticated (for example 401)

Case_Title: Product image upload works and bad files are refused
Steps_Summary: Upload a normal image, then try an oversize or wrong type file
Expected_Result: Good file is stored; bad file is rejected with a message a human can understand

Case_Title: Search and filters in the admin product list
Steps_Summary: Open the products list, type in search, apply filters
Expected_Result: The table only shows rows that match; the screen stays stable without client errors

Case_Title: Limited admin role versus full admin on sensitive actions
Steps_Summary: Using the runbook, try delete, export, or other risky actions with each account
Expected_Result: The lighter role is blocked where only full admins may act; the full role succeeds where the product allows it

Case_Title: Featured flags change what appears on the storefront
Steps_Summary: Turn featured or storefront-featured flags on or off and save
Expected_Result: Home or featured areas show the SKUs your product owner expects

Case_Title: Set primary category and see it on the storefront
Steps_Summary: In admin, assign categories to a product and set which one is primary, then view that product on the shop
Expected_Result: The storefront product page shows the correct category trail or breadcrumb

Case_Title: Stock edits in admin show on the live product page
Steps_Summary: Change stock in admin, then hard-refresh the product page on the shop
Expected_Result: In stock or out of stock labels and messages match what the API and rules say

Case_Title: Create a draft product then publish it
Steps_Summary: Create a minimal product as draft, save, then publish
Expected_Result: The product appears on the storefront at the expected address

Case_Title: Filter the orders list by status and date
Steps_Summary: Pick a status and a date range in the orders list filters
Expected_Result: Only matching orders appear and paging still works

Case_Title: Open an order change status and add a note
Steps_Summary: From the orders list, open one order, change its status, add an internal note
Expected_Result: The new status and note are saved and customer-visible rules from the PO still hold

Case_Title: Export or invoice depending on admin role
Steps_Summary: As each role type, try export or invoice actions described in the doc
Expected_Result: Panel users get 403 where forbidden; full users receive files where allowed

Case_Title: Create edit and retire shipping methods
Steps_Summary: As a full admin, add a method, edit it, disable it, and delete if allowed
Expected_Result: Checkout shipping quotes only list methods that should still be active

Case_Title: Site settings reject bad values and save good ones
Steps_Summary: Try saving an invalid setting key or value, then a valid one
Expected_Result: Invalid input is refused; valid values are stored and the storefront reads them when relevant

Case_Title: Who may change site settings or shipping admin APIs
Steps_Summary: Call settings or shipping admin routes with a panel account and with a full account
Expected_Result: Each call returns 403 or success exactly as the middleware documentation says

Case_Title: Jenkins job leaves downloadable test reports
Steps_Summary: Open the Jenkins job, download Surefire and Playwright report archives
Expected_Result: The zip or folders contain the test results you expect from that run

Case_Title: Production build must not leak secrets into JavaScript
Steps_Summary: Search the built JavaScript bundles for obvious secret patterns such as long API keys
Expected_Result: No real production secrets are embedded in files sent to visitors

Case_Title: Every new P0 story has a test row in the traceability sheet
Steps_Summary: Open the traceability spreadsheet and compare Story IDs to the release scope
Expected_Result: Each new P0 user story has at least one linked Playwright, API, or manual test row

Case_Title: Staging environment smoke test and one COD journey
Preconditions: PLAYWRIGHT_ORIGIN and API URLs for staging
Steps_Summary: Run the agreed smoke checklist and one cash-on-delivery path against staging URLs
Expected_Result: Results match local expectations except where differences are written down for staging
Run_Command: Manual plus Playwright against staging URLs
